title: Environmental Justice, Science and Technology
description: Learn about the environmental justice movement, its history, and how science and technology can be used to tackle environmental challenges and injustice.

title: Carbon Aware Computing for GenAI Developers
description: Learn how to perform model training and inference jobs with cleaner, low-carbon energy in the cloud!

title: Sustainable AI: Managing Environmental Impacts Responsibly
description: Understanding and reducing the environmental footprint of AI through sustainable decision-making.

title: Policy Impact for Emerging Climate Leaders
description: To support emerging climate leaders in their efforts to move climate policy, the Aspen Policy Academy is hosting a part-time, unpaid policy impact bootcamp for climate leaders between the ages of 21 and 30.

title: How Tech is Driving Sustainability
description: In this course, Talal Gedeon highlights the demand for individuals and organizations to design and implement effective sustainable and environmental technological solutions. Learn what the Internet of Things (IoT) is and how it can be used to promote a more sustainable future.

title: Green Digital Certificate
description:
INCO Academy’s free Green Digital Certificate online programs equip
you with the knowledge and skills to build sustainable digital solutions.

title: Sustainability Transformation for Leaders
description: The global business sector is being transformed around a common issue: environmental sustainability. Leaders should not only be prepared for the business transformations that lie ahead, but also be key architects of developing sustainability policies in the workplace.

title: AI & ML Applications in Oil and Gas Industry
description: This course is part of Petroleum Engineering with AI Applications Specialization on Coursera.

title: Sustainable AI
description: The four-week course equips you with tools, and strategies to mitigate AI’s climate impact, optimize AI products for sustainability, and implement responsible, energy-efficient AI technologies

title: Sustainable AI - Reducing Carbon Footprint and Optimizing Performance
description: This course will enable you to understand the environmental impact of AI applications, how to measure their carbon footprint, evaluate the right approach, and optimize your AI workloads to minimize their energy consumption.

title: An Introduction to AI and Sustainability
description: Amy Luers explores how artificial intelligence can drive the rapid and large-scale transformation needed to combat climate change and biodiversity loss, highlighting AI's role in reducing emissions, expanding renewable energy, minimizing waste, and protecting nature while emphasizing the importance of responsible AI development.

title: AI Skills Passport
description: Building on the success of the EY Microsoft Green Skills Passport program, the AI Skills Passport (AISP) has been created to assist students aged 16 and older in learning about AI technologies, working with them, and applying them to various industries and careers they choose to pursue.

title: INCO Academy
description: Passionate about sustainability? Our Green Pathways program equips you with the skills to start your career in the green sector, tailored to job seekers, women, and historically excluded groups.

title: Microsoft Sustainability Academy
description: Tune into Microsoft's Sustainability Academy to learn more about how the worlds of sustainability, ESG, and AI can come together.

title: AI for Energy Solutions
description: You will explore the intersection of artificial intelligence and climate solutions, with a focus on tackling real-world energy challenges.

title: CV4Ecology Workshop
description: A three-week intensive program teaching Computer Vision Methods for Ecology, seeking to empower ecologists to accurately and efficiently analyze large image, audio, or video datasets using computer vision.

title: OpenAI Academy
description: Check out OpenAI's Academy to learn more about various AI applications.

title: WILDLABS Conservation Courses
description: Browse our selection of self-led conservation technology learning resources. These include internal courses created and hosted on WILDLABS, as well as courses hosted externally.

title: Measuring AI's Environmental Impact: Technical Bootcamp
description: In this 2-week technical bootcamp, you'll master the practical skills needed to measure and track AI systems' environmental impact.

title: Introduction to Greenhouse Gas Accounting for IT
description: Expand your IT sustainability skills and dig into the environmental impacts of technology.

title: UN SDG Academy
description: Free educational resources from the world’s leading experts on sustainable development which includes some ML/AI aspects.

title: The Open University OpenLearn Academy
description: Free courses on sustainability, business, energy & AI.

title: MIT OpenCourseWare
description: Elite-level courses on climate, energy systems & innovation.

title: Harvard Online Learning
description: Free sustainability, leadership & AI courses.

title: FutureLearn
description: Climate, clean tech & energy transition courses from top universities.

title: AI and Climate Change
description: This course is a component of the AI for Good specialization, focusing on the ethical and impactful application of AI to address global challenges.

title: AI-Powered Course: Raising Funds for Your Business
description: With a target audience of SMEs and entrepreneurs, this course gives participants an overview of the processes involved in raising various kinds of funds for their activities. It begins by looking at a few techniques to evaluate financial need. Then, it explores the differences between equity, grants and debt (both long and short-term), and details the various capital providers from which SMEs might raise funds.

title: Introduction to AI Fluency
description: Learn to collaborate with AI systems effectively, efficiently, ethically, and safely from Anthropic.

title: Sustainable AI for Developers: Strategies, Techniques, and Best Practices
description: This comprehensive course explores design approaches to AI that balance environmental impact, innovation, and profitability. Instructor Fawad Qureshi delves into the concept of sustainable AI, exploring its significance and impact. Gain insights into the background and recent developments in this field, particularly with the rise of generative AI. The course provides a holistic understanding of AI's environmental footprint, emphasizing the importance of optimizing resource consumption and adopting sustainable practices. Following these best practices will not only save energy consumption but also reduce your AI expenses.

title: AI for Sustainable Future
description: A program that combines artificial intelligence with sustainable practices to create environmentally-conscious technological solutions.

title: Sustainable Cloud Engineering
description: This 4-week course covers sustainable cloud computing, focusing on eco-friendly architecture, energy-efficient deployments, and GreenOps strategies to reduce IT’s environmental impact.

title: Introduction to Machine Learning and Control for Building Energy Systems
description: The open-sourced EN.560.454.01.SP25 Introduction to Machine Learning and Control for Building Energy Systems Civil and Systems Engineering Department at Johns Hopkins University

title: AI Serving The Common Good
description: Artificial intelligence is disrupting our societies at breakneck speed. Presented as a promise of efficiency and innovation, it is profoundly transforming the way we work, communicate, produce, and even govern. But what are we really talking about? How do these systems work? Who designs, finances, and controls them? And under what conditions can they truly serve the public interest?

title: Essex Accounting and Global Development School – AI & Global Risk Track
description: Includes modules on AI for sustainable development, ESG reporting, and environmental risk accounting, tailored for students and professionals in global development.

title: AI in Agriculture: Practical Introductory Course
description:
This course, "AI in Agriculture: Practical Introductory Course", helps you understand how artificial intelligence (AI) is being used on farms. You do not need to be a tech expert to learn from this .
It is made for farmers, students, business owners, and anyone curious about smart farming. The course is free and beginner-friendly. We use simple words, real examples, and easy explanations.
